reasoning:
The baseline 30-day average for 'Strategic developments' in Trinidad and Tobago was 1.667 over the 360 days ending July 24, implying exactly 20 recorded events. The threshold to resolve YES currently requires a 30-day period to see at least 2 events (or 3, if a recent influx of events pushes the total up). 

On July 18, 2025, T&T declared a major nationwide State of Emergency (SoE) following a prison plot to assassinate officials. On July 28, the SoE was extended for 3 months. This emergency period has triggered large-scale arrests and weapon seizures. 

For the first resolution window (R1: July 11 - August 9), there is a structural advantage: any events occurring in late July that raise the trailing 360-day average by a fraction of a point will contribute a full 1 point to the 30-day total for R1. Because R1 captures all the highest-profile 'Strategic developments' of the current crisis (the declaration, the plot discovery, the extension, and initial mass sweeps), it is overwhelmingly likely to beat the average. 

For subsequent dates, the likelihood decreases as they miss the initial wave of declarations. R2 (August 3 - September 1) will relying on ongoing arrests and seizures under the SoE, remaining moderately likely to hit the threshold. R3 (October) corresponds broadly with the SoE's scheduled expiration, while R4 (December/January) represents a return to baseline activities. Adhering to the principle of increasing uncertainty over longer forecast horizons, probabilities smoothly converge toward 0.5 for more distant dates.
